name: Sphinx
description: The Sphinx of Giza is one of the most iconic symbols of ancient Egypt. A colossal statue carved from a single outcrop of limestone, it depicts a creature with the body of a lion and the head of a human, typically male and often associated with the pharaoh. The Sphinx is believed to have been built during the reign of Pharaoh Khafre (c. 2558-2532 BC) as part of his mortuary complex. Its primary function was likely to serve as a guardian of the necropolis, warding off evil and protecting the pharaoh's tomb. Symbolically, the lion represented royal power and strength, while the human head embodied intelligence and wisdom. The Sphinx's enigmatic gaze and imposing size have inspired awe and wonder for millennia, making it a enduring testament to the ingenuity and artistic prowess of the ancient Egyptians. The weathered condition of the monument speaks to the passage of time and the relentless forces of nature. Its presence continues to captivate visitors and scholars alike, offering a glimpse into the rich cultural and religious beliefs of a civilization that flourished along the Nile River. The Sphinx represents the harmonious blend of human intellect and animal strength, a powerful symbol of kingship and divine protection. Its secrets continue to be unearthed and debated by Egyptologists, ensuring that its legacy will endure for generations to come.
subtitle: Guardian of the Giza Plateau
appearance: The Sphinx has the body of a lion and the head of a human. The body is typically recumbent, with the paws outstretched. The human head is usually male and wears a royal headdress, such as the nemes. The entire structure is carved from a single block of limestone.
habitat: Typically found guarding important locations, such as tombs and temples, primarily in desert environments.
behavior: The Sphinx is often depicted as a guardian, challenging those who approach with riddles or tests of worthiness. Failure to answer correctly could result in death or being barred from passage. Otherwise, it is a silent and watchful protector.

keyMyths.title: The Riddle of the Sphinx
keyMyths.description: The Sphinx challenges travelers with a riddle: 'What walks on four legs in the morning, two legs at noon, and three in the evening?' The answer is man, who crawls as a baby, walks upright as an adult, and uses a cane in old age.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Necropolis
keyMyths.description: The Sphinx is believed to have been a guardian figure, protecting the tombs and temples of ancient Egypt from evil spirits and those who would desecrate them. Its imposing presence and formidable appearance served as a deterrent.
keyMyths.source: Egyptian Religious Texts and Archaeological Context

sources.author: Mark Lehner
sources.description: A comprehensive study of the pyramids of Egypt, including information on the Sphinx.
sources.text: A comprehensive study of the pyramids of Egypt, including information on the Sphinx.

sources.author: Zahi Hawass
sources.work: The Secrets of the Sphinx: Restoring the Face of an Ancient Legend
sources.description: Details the restoration efforts and historical significance of the Sphinx.
sources.text: Details the restoration efforts and historical significance of the Sphinx.
